summary: Iteration 2 full-stack testing complete. Tested JWT auth, admin dashboard, protected endpoints, brute-force, and new UI sections (LoadingScreen, InstagramFeed, DishCarousel, Est. 1980 branding). Backend 17/18 pytest passes; brute-force lockout test failed due to K8s ingress load-balancing client.host across multiple internal proxy IPs. All frontend flows including login, dashboard tabs, refresh, logout, and reload-persistence passed.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout uses request.client.host which behind Kubernetes ingress observes internal proxy pod IPs (e.g. 10.219.0.197 and 10.219.0.204 alternating). Same real user across ~6 attempts gets identifier split across multiple pods → count never reaches 5 on any single identifier → 429 never triggers reliably in the deployed environment. In isolation the logic is correct (count>=5 → 429), but effectively it takes ~5*N attempts where N=number of proxy hops. Recommend using X-Forwarded-For (first IP) instead of request.client.host to identify real client.

action_items: Fix brute-force IP detection: read X-Forwarded-For header (first entry) in POST /api/auth/login instead of request.client.host, so ingress-forwarded requests are counted against the real client IP. Otherwise the 5-attempts-per-15-min lockout is effectively bypassed by K8s ingress load balancing.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: brute-force uses request.client.host which is a proxy IP under K8s ingress — need X-Forwarded-For

server.py: locked_until is refreshed on EVERY failed attempt (even attempt 1). Minor: means lockout window slides forward — attacker who waits 14 min between attempts stays locked. Acceptable behavior but non-standard.
server.py: no rate limit on public POST endpoints (/api/reservations, /api/contact, /api/newsletter) — spam risk

server.py: CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — invalid CORS combo (browsers will reject). Since JWT is in Authorization header not cookies, this works, but the config is fragile.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed rows in reservations/contact/newsletter collections via public POST endpoints for admin dashboard verification. Also cleared db.login_attempts before backend test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 passed all UI + all backend tests except brute-force lockout, which failed due to K8s ingress presenting multiple proxy source IPs (10.219.0.197 / 10.219.0.204) — this is a real deployment concern, not a test-script bug. To reproduce: run pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py::TestBruteForce and inspect db.login_attempts — multiple identifiers get created for the same client. Fix by using request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for', '').split(',')[0].strip() or request.client.host for the identifier.
rca of the issue: Root cause: FastAPI's request.client.host returns the immediate TCP peer IP. Under Kubernetes ingress + service load-balancing, the backend pod sees the internal proxy pod IP, and that varies across requests (round-robin/random). Identifier = f'{ip}:{email}' → different identifiers per request → each identifier's count increments independently, never crossing 5 within a single test run of 6 attempts. Mitigation: use X-Forwarded-For header's leftmost value as the client IP for lockout identifier.
